title: What to know about Matricaria chamomilla before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Matricaria chamomilla are its application in treating anxiety through the action of apigenin, a flavonoid that binds to GABA receptors, thereby reducing anxiety levels. Additionally, the plant's essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like bisabolol, which is effective in soothing digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome. Chamomilla's sedative properties, attributed to the sesquiterpene lactone matricarin, make it an effective treatment for insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness. Matricarin also contributes to the plant's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing uterine contractions. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of Matricaria chamomilla, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, make it effective in treating skin irritations, inflammation, stress, sleep disorders, allergies, and other conditions caused by inflammation.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Matricaria chamomilla are multifaceted, with its antimicrobial activity attributed to volatile oils, allowing it to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi. This plant also exhibits antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative damage, a process that is thought to be mediated by flavonoids and other phenolic compounds. Its anti-inflammatory properties are also well-documented, with apigenin and luteolin, two flavonoids present in Matricaria chamomilla, shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, the plant's antispasmodic activity is thought to be responsible for its ability to relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms, which may contribute to its anxiolytic and sedative effects, and its analgesic properties, which may be due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. Furthermore, Matricaria chamomilla has been found to have anti-allergic, hepatoprotective, and glycemic properties, with its anti-allergic activity attributed to the presence of apigenin and other flavonoids, while its hepatoprotective effects may be due to the scavenging of free radicals and the inhibition of lipid peroxidation.
identification: Matricaria chamomilla is a small, annual herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae), characterized by a slender stem (up to 30 cm tall) with bipinnate leaves, and small, white, daisy-like flowers (to 2 cm in diameter) with 20-40 florets, bearing a prominent, yellow center. This plant is native to Europe and Western Asia, but has been naturalized in many parts of the world, including North America, Australia, and New Zealand, and is commonly found in dry, sandy soils and along roadsides. Matricaria chamomilla is a prolific seed producer, with seeds germinating in as little as 10 days and the plant flowering within 6-8 weeks. It is a low-growing, spreading plant that can form dense mats, and is often associated with disturbed or cultivated areas. The plant's habit is often stoloniferous, with long, branching stems that produce new plants from adventitious roots.
cultivation: Matricaria chamomilla is a hardy annual herb that exhibits a spreading and ascending growth habit, typically reaching heights of 20-30 cm. It thrives in well-drained soil with a slightly acidic to neutral pH (5.5-7.0) and full sun to partial shade, requiring approximately 4-6 hours of direct sunlight per day. To promote optimal growth, it is essential to provide adequate moisture, with consistent moisture levels throughout the growing season, though the plant can tolerate some drought. Regular harvesting of the flowers and foliage can help control the plant's height and encourage bushy growth, while also stimulating root development. A balanced fertilizer application, ideally at the 1-2 leaf stage, can support the plant's growth and overall health throughout its cultivation cycle.
history: Matricaria chamomilla is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for thousands of years, with evidence of its use dating back to ancient Egypt and Greece. The plant's flowers and leaves were used to treat a variety of ailments, including insomnia, anxiety, and menstrual cramps, due to their content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties. In the 19th century, Matricaria chamomilla was used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, as well as to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever. The plant's antispasmodic properties were also utilized to treat muscle spasms and colic in infants. Its efficacy was documented in the German Commission E's monographs, which recognized its use as a calming agent and anti-inflammatory, but noted potential side effects and interactions with other medications.

constituents.name: Apigenin
constituents.description: Flavonoid compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-anxiety properties.

constituents.name: Luteolin
constituents.description: A flavonoid compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid glycoside constituent of Matricaria chamomilla, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-allergic properties.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid constituent of Matricaria chamomilla, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Chlorogenic acid
constituents.description: Chlorogenic acid is an ester of caffeic acid and 3,4-dihydroxyphenylacetic acid, found in Matricaria chamomilla with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Apigenin-7-O-glucoside
constituents.description: Apigenin-7-O-glucoside is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and estrogenic properties.

constituents.name: Matricarin
constituents.description: Matricarin is a sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a class of triterpene glycosides found in Matricaria chamomilla, characterized by their foaming, hemolytic and cytotoxic properties.

constituents.name: α-Bisabolol
constituents.description: α-Bisabolol is a sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and soothing properties.

constituents.name: Furanocoumarins
constituents.description: Furanocoumarins, found in Matricaria chamomilla, are phototoxic furanocoumarin compounds, which possess spasmolytic, anti-inflammatory and antiallergic properties

constituents.name: Luteolin-7-O-glucoside
constituents.description: Luteolin-7-O-glucoside is a flavonoid glycoside found in Matricaria chamomilla with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Bisabolol
constituents.description: Bisabolol is a tricyclic sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Apigetrin
constituents.description: Apigetrin is an apigenin glycoside, a flavonoid compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Chamazulene
constituents.description: A blue-colored, oily constituent of Matricaria chamomilla, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

constituents.name: Alpha-bisabolol
constituents.description: A sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anti-oxidant properties.

constituents.name: Essential oils
constituents.description: Matricaria chamomilla essential oils contain alpha-bisabolol, with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids in Matricaria chamomilla are flavonoids and hydroxycinnamic acids, which are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Caffeic acid
constituents.description: Caffeic acid is a phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Naringenin
constituents.description: Flavonoid, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective flavanone found in the flowers of Matricaria chamomilla.

constituents.name: Volatile oils
constituents.description: Matricaria chamomilla's volatile oils are primarily composed of azulene, bisabolol, and chamazulene, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Allergic reactions can be exacerbated in individuals with sensitivity to plants in the Asteraceae family.
In susceptible individuals, Matricaria chamomilla ingestion can trigger allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis.
Matricaria chamomilla interacts with anticoagulant medications, increasing the risk of bleeding.
Matricaria chamomilla can inhibit platelet aggregation which may hinder blood clotting.
Severe allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, have been reported in rare cases.
Matricaria chamomilla extracts can cause irritability and restlessness in some individuals.
Allergic reactions to Matricaria chamomilla can be exacerbated in individuals with allergies or sensitivities to the plant.
Matricaria chamomilla inhibits the enzyme acetylcholinesterase, reducing the breakdown of acetylcholine.
Matricaria chamomilla may cause anaphylactic reactions, which can interfere with surgical procedures.

intro_description: Matricaria chamomilla, commonly known as German chamomile, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions. Its main medicinal uses include anxiety disorders, insomnia, digestive issues, and inflammation. The herb has main health benefits such as reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Matricaria chamomilla's main therapeutic properties include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic effects. The herb contains main bioactive compounds like apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene. People often make a herbal tea by infusing dried flowers in hot water.

uses_list.description: Matricaria chamomilla is used for anxiety due to its sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which are attributed to the presence of apigenin and luteolin, flavonoids that have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and reduce stress levels.

Matricaria chamomilla is used for insomnia as it helps to promote relaxation and reduce symptoms of insomnia, including restlessness and difficulty falling asleep, due to its sedative properties and ability to calm the nervous system.

Matricaria chamomilla is used for digestive issues, such as upset stomach, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive tract, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms.

Matricaria chamomilla is used for stress due to its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to adapt to stress and promote balance in the body, as well as its ability to reduce cortisol levels and promote relaxation.

Matricaria chamomilla is used for skin irritation, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the skin, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and inflammation, and promote healing.

Matricaria chamomilla is used for inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with inflammatory conditions.
Matricaria chamomilla is used for nausea due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive tract, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

Matricaria chamomilla is used for allergies due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the skin and respiratory tract, which can help to reduce symptoms of allergies, such as itching, redness, and congestion.

Matricaria chamomilla is used for menstrual cramps due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the uterus, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.

Matricaria chamomilla is used for eczema due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the skin, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and inflammation, and promote healing and relief from symptoms associated with eczema.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Matricaria chamomilla is used to treat anxiety through its anxiolytic properties, while also addressing insomnia by promoting relaxation and regulating sleep patterns. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in soothing digestive issues, such as inflammatory bowel disease. Furthermore, its adaptogenic effects help alleviate stress and inflammation, reducing the risk of skin irritation and promoting wound healing. Matricaria chamomilla is also used to alleviate nausea and menstrual cramps, as well as treat allergies and eczema. The plant's anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties are also beneficial in treating muscle spasms, gastroenteritis, skin irritations, diarrhea, and rheumatism, in addition to providing pain relief and alleviating symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux, hives, and urticaria.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Matricaria chamomilla is used to treat anxiety, digestive issues, insomnia, stress, and skin irritation by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. It is also employed to alleviate nausea, menstrual cramps, allergies, eczema, rheumatism, and headaches through its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla is used to treat muscle spasms, diarrhea, gastroenteritis, hives, and gastroesophageal reflux by calming the digestive system and reducing irritation. Furthermore, it is used to alleviate migraines, colic, and skin irritations by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. By addressing these conditions, Matricaria chamomilla has become a valuable herb in traditional chinese medicine.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Matricaria chamomilla is used to treat anxiety, stress, insomnia, and inflammation by reducing the production of stress hormones and promoting relaxation. It is also used to alleviate digestive issues, nausea, and allergies by soothing the digestive tract and suppressing the release of histamine. Additionally, it is effective in treating skin irritations, eczema, and wounds by promoting the healing of damaged tissues and reducing inflammation. Furthermore, it is used to relieve menstrual cramps, headaches, rheumatism, muscle spasms, and gastroesophageal reflux by reducing pain and inflammation. Moreover, it is employed to treat diarrhea, gastroenteritis, sleep disorders, and digestion problems by regulating bowel movements and improving digestion.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Matricaria chamomilla is used to treat insomnia by promoting relaxation, anxiety through its anxiolytic properties, and digestive issues due to its carminative effects. It is also applied externally to alleviate skin irritations and menstrual cramps, and internally to treat allergies, inflammation, nausea, stress, diarrhea, eczema, and rheumatism. Furthermore, the plant is used to manage muscle spasms, gastroenteritis, fever, hives, wounds, and vomiting, while also providing relief from gastritis and allergic reactions. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties make it an effective treatment for wounds and gastroenteritis, and its antihistamine properties help in managing allergic reactions and hives. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla is used to treat eczema, a chronic skin condition characterized by inflammation and irritation.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Matricaria chamomilla is used to treat anxiety, insomnia and stress by promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness. This plant extract is also commonly used to alleviate skin irritation, inflammation, and digestive issues, as well as nausea and allergies. It is particularly effective in addressing menstrual cramps, headaches, and gastroenteritis, and is also used to alleviate colic, rheumatism, and muscle spasms. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla has been shown to reduce the severity of hives, diarrhea, and digestion issues, and is used to promote wound healing and alleviate muscle tension. Furthermore, it has been found to be effective in reducing skin irritations and relieving symptoms of gastroenteritis, colic and rheumatism.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains apigenin, an flavonoid that has been shown to have anxiolytic effects by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, leading to a reduction in anxiety symptoms.

The plant's high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate related symptoms.

The plant's essential oils, particularly chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

The plant's essential oils, particularly chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation.

The plant's flavonoids, particularly apigenin, have been found to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced insomnia symptoms.

The plant's essential oils, particularly alpha-bisabolol, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe menstrual cramps and alleviate symptoms of dysmenorrhea.
The plant's flavonoids, particularly apigenin, have been found to have anxiolytic and sedative effects, helping to reduce stress levels and promote a sense of calm and relaxation.

The plant's flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of allergic reactions such as itching, redness, and swelling.

The plant's flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, which can help to prevent cancer cell growth and reduce the risk of certain types of cancer.
The plant's flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, have been found to have immunomodulatory properties, which can help to boost the immune system and reduce the risk of infections.

benefits_description: Matricaria chamomilla reduces anxiety by containing apigenin, a flavonoid that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting a calming effect. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its high content of luteolin and apigenin, relieve inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Matricaria chamomilla treats digestive issues by soothing the stomach and intestines with its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which also help to regulate bowel movements. The plant soothes skin irritation by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, and its calming effects calm insomnia symptoms by regulating sleep patterns and promoting relaxation. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla eases menstrual cramps by relaxing smooth muscle and reducing prostaglandin production, decreases stress levels by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, treats allergic reactions by inhibiting histamine release, prevents cancer growth by suppressing cancer cell proliferation, and boosts the immune system by activating natural killer cells and enhancing phagocytosis.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Matricaria chamomilla lower blood pressure by exerting vasodilatory effects, thereby reducing peripheral resistance and cardiac workload. The anti-inflammatory properties of Matricaria chamomilla reduce inflammation, which in turn improves circulation by promoting the removal of inflammatory mediators from the vascular system. Matricaria chamomilla enhance vasodilation, relax blood vessels, and prevent blood clots by inhibiting platelet aggregation, which collectively increase red blood cells and oxygen delivery to tissues. The antioxidant properties of Matricaria chamomilla regulate heart rate and reduce stress response by modulating the sympathetic nervous system, preventing arrhythmias and cardiac arrhythmias. By increasing oxygen delivery and regulating heart rate, Matricaria chamomilla prevent blood clots and promote a healthy circulatory system.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Matricaria chamomilla relieves inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and protect the digestive system from oxidative damage. This plant also soothes irritations in the digestive tract, such as those caused by ulcers, due to its protective and regenerative effects on the mucous membranes. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla eases digestion by stimulating digestive enzymes and enhancing gut motility, thereby facilitating the breakdown and absorption of nutrients. Furthermore, its anti-diarrheal and anti-nausea properties calm diarrhea and reduce nausea, respectively, while its spasmolytic effects prevent spasms in the gastrointestinal tract. Moreover, Matricaria chamomilla heals ulcers, suppresses gas, tames colitis, and stabilizes the gut microbiota due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, ultimately promoting a balanced and healthy digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Matricaria chamomilla regulates hormones by exerting its estrogen-like effect through the plant's isoflavones, which bind to estrogen receptors and mimic the body's natural estrogen. Reducing stress is another benefit, achieved through its antioxidant properties, which protect cells from oxidative damage and promote a sense of calm by modulating the body's GABA receptors. Balancing adrenaline is facilitated by the plant's adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and maintain homeostasis. The herb also stabilizes the thyroid by reducing inflammation and promoting the production of thyroid hormones, and boosts insulin by activating the body's AMPK pathway and increasing glucose uptake in cells. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla calms ovarian function by reducing prostaglandin production, supports pituitary function by stimulating the release of prolactin, soothes the pancreas by reducing inflammation, modulates cortisol by inhibiting the activity of 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, and protects the adrenal glands from oxidative damage by its antioxidant activity.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Matricaria chamomilla soothes skin due to its anti-inflammatory properties, calming irritated skin and reducing redness, while also possessing antimicrobial properties that prevent the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi. This plant also reduces irritation in the skin, resulting from its soothing and calming effects, allowing it to be an effective treatment for conditions like eczema, where it calms eczema symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla heals wounds due to its antioxidant properties, which protect the wound from oxidative damage and promote the growth of new tissue. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote relaxation of muscles, reducing muscle spasms and tension. Matricaria chamomilla also sedates inflammation and treats acne by reducing sebum production and preventing the growth of Propionibacterium acnes bacteria, removes scars by promoting collagen synthesis and tissue repair, and cures burns by protecting the skin from damage and promoting the growth of new tissue.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Matricaria chamomilla stimulates lymph flow by activating the lymphatic vessels, which enhances lymph function and accelerates lymph drainage. The antimicrobial properties of Matricaria chamomilla boost lymphatic activity, allowing it to reduce lymph swelling and promote lymph circulation. Matricaria chamomilla's anti-inflammatory properties support lymph health by relaxing lymph tissues and improving lymph filtration. Additionally, its antioxidant properties increase lymph production, which, in turn, enhances lymph function and promotes lymph circulation. As a result, Matricaria chamomilla's medicinal properties work synergistically to maintain a healthy lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Matricaria chamomilla relaxes muscles due to its relaxant and antispasmodic properties, which soothe muscle spasms and reduce muscle tension. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Matricaria chamomilla reduce inflammation in the musculoskeletal system, thereby easing joint pain. Matricaria chamomilla also soothes tendons and calms ligaments, while its anti-swelling properties decrease swelling in the affected areas. The anti-inflammatory properties of Matricaria chamomilla lower muscle tension, improve bone density, and stabilize joints, reducing the risk of muscle damage and joint instability. Regular use of Matricaria chamomilla has been shown to prevent muscle damage by protecting muscle fibers from oxidative stress and inflammation.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Matricaria chamomilla reduces stress through its flavonoid and apigenin content, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. Improving sleep is another benefit of Matricaria chamomilla, as its apigenin and luteolin properties act as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing sleep latency. Calming nerves is a result of Matricaria chamomilla's anti-anxiety properties, attributed to its apigenin and luteolin content, which have anxiolytic and sedative effects. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of Matricaria chamomilla also relax muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramping. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla soothes anxiety, eases fatigue, enhances focus, promotes relaxation, reduces insomnia, and boosts mood due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and adaptogenic properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for nervous system health.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Matricaria chamomilla relaxes uterus, reducing muscle spasms and preventing preterm labor due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also help reduce inflammation in the reproductive system, alleviating conditions such as endometriosis and pelvic inflammatory disease. Easing menstrual cramps, regulating hormones, and increasing fertility are also benefits associated with Matricaria chamomilla, which contains apigenin and luteolin that exhibit estrogen-like activity and anti-androgenic effects, toning the reproductive system and regulating hormonal imbalances. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla relieves cramping, decreases anxiety, and enhances libido by reducing stress and promoting relaxation due to its anxiolytic and sedative properties. By regulating cycles, Matricaria chamomilla helps stabilize menstrual cycles, preventing irregularities and promoting a healthy reproductive system.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Matricaria chamomilla reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing congestion by thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion from the respiratory tract. Easing congestion enables relaxed airways, allowing for smoother breathing and alleviating wheezing. The anti-spasmodic properties of Matricaria chamomilla relax airways, reducing spasms and relieving coughs, while soothing coughs and calming asthma symptoms. Soothing coughs also contribute to healing lungs by reducing irritation and inflammation. Furthermore, the plant's antimicrobial and antioxidant properties treat bronchitis by protecting the bronchi from oxidative stress and infections.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Matricaria chamomilla relieves urinary tract infections due to its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens, and reduces kidney inflammation by its anti-inflammatory properties that minimize oxidative stress and tissue damage. Matricaria chamomilla prevents bladder irritation by its antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and protect the urothelial lining from damage, and cures kidney stones by its diuretic properties that increase urine production and facilitate stone expulsion. Matricaria chamomilla also prevents urinary incontinence by its relaxing properties that soothe the urethral sphincter and maintain continence, reduces bladder spasms by its analgesic properties that alleviate pain and discomfort, and relaxes urinary muscles by its antispasmodic properties that calm muscle contractions and improve bladder function. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla tones urinary organs by its adaptogenic properties that regulate the autonomic nervous system and optimize organ function, and prevents kidney damage by its protective properties that shield the kidneys from toxins and oxidative stress. Matricaria chamomilla's combination of these medicinal properties makes it a valuable natural remedy for urinary system health.

properties_list.description: Matricaria chamomilla has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain bacteria, including Escherichia coli, Staphylococcus aureus, and Candida albicans, due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its essential oil.

Matricaria chamomilla possesses antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, and other phenolic compounds that can scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress.

The anti-inflammatory properties of Matricaria chamomilla are primarily attributed to the presence of apigenin and other flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis and eczema.

Matricaria chamomilla has been found to exhibit analgesic properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its essential oil that can interact with pain receptors and reduce pain perception.
The anxiolytic properties of Matricaria chamomilla are attributed to the presence of apigenin and other flavonoids that can interact with GABA receptors and reduce anxiety and stress levels.

Matricaria chamomilla has been found to exhibit sedative properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its essential oil that can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety and stress levels.

The antispasmodic properties of Matricaria chamomilla are primarily attributed to the presence of apigenin and other flavonoids that can relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms in the gastrointestinal tract and other areas.

Matricaria chamomilla has been found to exhibit immunomodulatory properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its essential oil that can modulate the immune response and increase the production of cytokines and other immune cells.

The cytoprotective properties of Matricaria chamomilla are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other phenolic compounds that can protect against cell damage and promote cellular regeneration and proliferation.

Matricaria chamomilla has been found to exhibit hepatoprotective properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of flavonoids and other phenolic compounds that can protect against liver damage and promote liver regeneration and function.

parts_description: The most used parts of Matricaria chamomilla for medicinal purposes are the flowers, which contain high amounts of apigenin, a flavonoid, and bisabolol, a sesquiterpene alcohol, as well as volatile oils, particularly alpha-bisabolol and chamazulene, responsible for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds exhibit analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, making the flowers useful in treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and anxiety. The volatile oils in the flowers also possess antioxidant and anti-allergic properties, which contribute to their use in soothing skin irritations and promoting wound healing. The flavonoids present in the flowers, such as apigenin, have been shown to have a protective effect on the nervous system and to exhibit anti-anxiety and sedative properties. The combination of these bioactive compounds makes the flowers of Matricaria chamomilla a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant are rich in flavonoids, apigenin, luteolin, and sesquiterpene lactones, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antispasmodic, and antioxidant properties, providing relief from insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties to soothe digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations, and as a sleep aid and anxiolytic.
preparations.preparation_overview: Matricaria chamomilla tincture is commonly used to treat anxiety, insomnia, menstrual cramps, stress, skin irritations, digestive issues, muscle spasms, nausea, allergies, and gastroesophageal reflux. It's a widely used herbal preparation, often found in pharmacies and health food stores. Its effects include promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, making it a common treatment for various health issues. Matricaria chamomilla tincture is typically made from the flowers of the plant, which are steeped in a solvent, usually ethanol, to extract their bioactive compounds. The process of making the tincture is moderately difficult, requiring attention to detail and precise measurements to ensure the desired strength and quality.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned flowers with a solvent, such as vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing the mixture to steep for several weeks.
Strain the liquid from the jar through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Discard the solids and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle, sealed tightly.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, and to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations, including inflammation and eczema.
preparations.preparation_overview: Matricaria chamomilla essential oil is used to treat insomnia, anxiety, nausea, eczema, rashes, skin irritation, digestive issues and headaches. It is a commonly used herbal essential oil. Its effects are often characterized by a calming and soothing sensation. The oil is derived from the flowers of the Matricaria chamomilla plant, which is a popular ingredient for aromatherapy and herbal remedies. The process of making the oil is moderately complex, requiring careful distillation to extract the essential compounds from the flowers.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh Matricaria chamomilla flowers from a clean and pesticide-free area.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area to preserve their essential oils.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to heat the dried flowers and release their essential oils.
Collect the released essential oils by condensing the steam and separating the oil from the water.
Filter the obtained essential oil to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat mild inflammation of the rectum, relieve itching, and reduce pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, anal fissures, and proctitis.
preparations.preparation_overview: Matricaria chamomilla suppository is a herbal medication used to treat various conditions including menstrual cramps, irritable bowel syndrome, diarrhea, inflammatory bowel disease, anal fissures, skin irritation, proctitis, urethritis, and vaginosis. It's an uncommonly used preparation, likely due to its limited availability and the relatively low number of reported studies. The suppository has a unique effect, which is often described as anti-inflammatory and soothing. Matricaria chamomilla suppositories are made with dried and processed flowers of the chamomile plant, which are then incorporated into a suppository base. The preparation requires precise processing and standardization of the plant material, making it hard to manufacture consistently.
preparation_recipe: Gather Matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them completely in a cool, dark place.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a suitable base material such as cocoa butter or vegetable shortening to create a uniform consistency.
Mold the mixture into small suppository shapes and let them cool and harden completely.
Store the herbal suppositories in a cool, dark place and use them as needed.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, commonly used to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns, and to reduce itching and inflammation associated with eczema and dermatitis.
preparations.preparation_overview: Matricaria chamomilla salve is a herbal remedy used to treat various skin conditions including sunburn, burns, skin irritation, wound healing, rash, eczema, insect bites, acne, herpes, and psoriasis. This preparation is commonly used due to its proven efficacy in soothing and calming the skin. One of the common effects of using Matricaria chamomilla salve is its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness and promote healing. It is typically made from the flowers of the Matricaria chamomilla plant, which contain compounds like apigenin and luteolin that contribute to its therapeutic properties. However, producing high-quality Matricaria chamomilla salve can be challenging due to the need for precise extraction and distillation techniques to ensure the optimal concentration of bioactive compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather Matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them thoroughly to preserve their potency.
Infuse the dried flowers in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, by placing them in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the oil to absorb the herbal properties.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate it from the dried flowers, and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with a beeswax or candelilla wax to create a solid salve, and heat the mixture gently to melt the wax and combine it with the oil.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is traditionally used to treat insomnia, anxiety, restlessness, and as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent, also used to soothe gastrointestinal issues and skin irritations.
preparations.preparation_overview: Matricaria chamomilla tea is a popular herbal remedy used to treat a range of conditions including digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome and nausea, insomnia, skin issues, anxiety, stress, menstrual cramps, restlessness and inflammation. It is one of the most widely used herbal teas, found in many health food stores and online. Its widespread use is likely due to its consistent effect of promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. The tea is made by infusing the flowers of the Matricaria chamomilla plant in hot water. This simple process makes it easy to prepare at home, allowing users to enjoy the benefits of the tea in the comfort of their own surroundings.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers from a trusted source or your own garden.
Rinse the flowers gently with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the Matricaria chamomilla flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, or until the desired strength of tea is reached.
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy hot or cold, sweetened with honey if desired.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness, and to soothe digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome, inflammation, and menstrual cramps.
preparations.preparation_overview: Matricaria chamomilla capsule is used to treat insomnia, anxiety, digestive issues, skin inflammation, stress, menstrual cramps, allergies, and muscle spasms. This herbal capsule is a very common remedy, widely used due to its effectiveness in alleviating various health issues. Its widespread usage is largely due to its ability to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. The capsule is made from the flowers of the Matricaria chamomilla plant, which are harvested and processed into a convenient and easily consumable form. The manufacturing process of Matricaria chamomilla capsule is moderately complex, requiring careful processing and standardization to ensure consistent quality and potency.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers from a trusted source.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Mix the powder with a suitable binding agent, such as gelatin or starch, to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared blend of Matricaria chamomilla powder and binding agent.
Seal the capsules using a capsule filling machine or by hand to prevent the contents from spilling out.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Matricaria chamomilla are inducing allergic reactions and exacerbate conditions such as asthma and eczema in sensitive individuals, while also triggering anaphylaxis in rare cases. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla may interact with certain medications, like blood thinners and diabetes medications, leading to decreased efficacy or adverse reactions. In some cases, it can hinder wound healing by prolonging the inflammatory response, whereas in pregnant women, it may worsen the symptoms of preterm labor and birth complications. Furthermore, using Matricaria chamomilla improperly can provoke gastrointestinal upset, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, in some people, and aggravate peptic ulcers in vulnerable individuals. In rare instances, it may inhibit the body's ability to metabolize certain substances, including other herbal supplements, while also interfering with the effectiveness of certain medical treatments, such as vaccines and immunosuppressants.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Pregnant women should consult a doctor before using Matricaria chamomilla due to potential uterine stimulation.
Take the exact amount recommended to avoid adverse effects.
Matricaria chamomilla may cause uterine contractions and stimulate the uterus, which can lead to miscarriage or premature labor, and its active compounds are excreted in breast milk.
Chamomile may lower blood sugar levels and interact with diabetes medications, so glucose levels should be monitored.
Matricaria chamomilla essential oil may cause allergic reactions in some individuals, especially those with ragweed allergy.
It may exacerbate bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties.
Due to potential interactions with medications and exacerbation of certain health conditions, use under medical supervision.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid in kidney or liver disease
precautions.precaution_list_description: Matricaria chamomilla may cause increased risk of bleeding or alter liver function, exacerbating kidney or liver disease.
Chamomile extracts inhibit cytochrome P450 enzymes, increasing levels of certain medications such as sedatives, and decreasing levels of others like anticoagulants.
Inform healthcare provider of drowsiness, diarrhea, or stomach upset.

intro_study: According to a study published by Phytotherapy research: PTR, Matricaria chamomilla has been found to have positive effects on blood pressure and heart rate in both normotensive and hypertensive individuals. The study revealed that Matricaria chamomilla extracts significantly reduced systolic and diastolic blood pressure, and heart rate in both animal and human subjects. Additionally, the extract was found to improve serum biochemical parameters and lipid profile levels, as well as restore the amount of reduced Glutathione in tissues and increase the activity of superoxide dismutase.

According to a study published by "Journal of Cancer", Matricaria chamomilla has been found to exhibit good antioxidant and anticancer activities, with its ethyl acetate extract showing the highest anticancer activity. This suggests that Matricaria chamomilla may be a valuable source of natural anti-cancer compounds for the treatment and management of various cancers. Additionally, the study's findings indicate that Matricaria chamomilla's extracts may have potential therapeutic applications in the management of prostate cancer.

According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Matricaria chamomilla essential oils have been found to have several positive effects. Specifically, Matricaria chamomilla was shown to exhibit antioxidant activity, with its essential oils and honey mixture displaying a high radical scavenging activity. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla essential oils were found to possess antidiabetic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them a potential target for treating diabetes and inflammation.

According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", the roots of Matricaria chamomilla exhibit antioxidant activity, with polar extracts showing IC50 values ranging from 13 to 57 µg/mL in the DPPH radical scavenging assay, similar to that of its flower extracts. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla root extracts demonstrated superoxide radical scavenging potential and mild antibacterial effects against certain bacteria. Furthermore, the study highlights the potential of Matricaria chamomilla roots as a sustainable source of bioactive phytochemicals, previously discarded as a by-product of flower production.

According to a study published by "EXCLI journal", Matricaria chamomilla has been found to have certain positive effects in the prevention of obesity and complications of diabetes. The study suggests that the preparations from chamomile flowers, rich in phenolic compounds and essential oils, can improve gastrointestinal function and demonstrate antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial actions. These effects were associated with modulation of signaling pathways involving key transcription factors, such as AMP-activated protein kinase, NF-κB, Nrf2, and PPARγ, which may contribute to its anti-obesity effects.

According to a study published by "Microbiological research", Matricaria chamomilla has been found to have numerous positive effects, particularly due to its high content of valuable components such as α-bisabolol and chamazulene. These compounds contribute to its antibacterial properties, making it effective in traditional medicine and as a food preservative. Additionally, its bioactivity has been noted to be influenced by various factors, including the plant part, origin, and quality of the source, as well as the season of harvest and methods of extraction.

According to a study published by "BMC research notes", Matricaria chamomilla has been found to exhibit potent antioxidant activity, with a high percentage inhibition of DPPH scavenging activity ranging from 84.2% to 94.8% at different concentrations. Additionally, it has been shown to possess high levels of polyphenols and flavonoids, which contribute to its antioxidant properties. Furthermore, the study revealed that Matricaria chamomilla can inhibit the growth of human hepatoma (HepG2) cancer cells, suggesting its potential as a cancer preventive agent.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Matricaria chamomilla are exhibited through its antimicrobial activity, which helps combat bacterial and fungal infections, while its antioxidant properties prevent oxidative stress and cell damage. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of this plant alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the body, making it an effective treatment for various musculoskeletal disorders. Its anxiolytic and sedative effects help calm the mind, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, while its antispasmodic properties relieve muscle spasms and cramps. Additionally, the immunomodulatory and cytoprotective properties of Matricaria chamomilla help regulate the immune system and protect cells from damage, respectively. The hepatoprotective effects of this plant also safeguard the liver from toxins and damage, making it a valuable treatment for various liver-related disorders.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Matricaria chamomilla are apigenin, a flavone with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, luteolin, a flavone with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, quercetin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, kaempferol, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and chlorogenic acid, a polyphenol with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, all of which contribute to the plant's therapeutic effects, particularly in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and are responsible for its anxiolytic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. Matricaria chamomilla's constituents have been shown to modulate the body's response to stress, reduce inflammation and pain, and exhibit neuroprotective effects. Apigenin has been demonstrated to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, while quercetin has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Chlorogenic acid has been found to have a protective effect on the gut and has been used to treat various gastrointestinal disorders. The combination of these constituents in Matricaria chamomilla makes it a valuable remedy for treating a range of health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and gastrointestinal disorders.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Matricaria chamomilla for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are liquid extracts used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to their apigenin and luteolin content. The essential oil, obtained through steam distillation, is used topically to soothe skin irritations, such as eczema and dermatitis, and is also used as a calming agent in aromatherapy. A suppository, made by infusing the herb in a fatty base, is used to treat menstrual cramps, uterine spasms, and other gynecological issues. Salves, which are topical creams or ointments, are applied directly to the skin to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, utilizing the plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Matricaria chamomilla is also available in capsule form, which is often used to treat gastrointestinal issues, including upset stomach, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Matricaria chamomilla medicinally are consulting a doctor before use, especially for patients with underlying health conditions or taking medications. It is essential to follow dosage instructions carefully to avoid adverse reactions and to achieve the desired therapeutic effect. Matricaria chamomilla should be avoided in pregnancy and breastfeeding due to its potential effects on the fetus or baby. Additionally, individuals with bleeding disorders should use Matricaria chamomilla under medical supervision and monitor their blood sugar levels to prevent interactions with antidiabetic medications. Furthermore, patients with kidney or liver disease should report any side effects of Matricaria chamomilla to their doctor, and those with allergies should be cautious when using it, while also being aware that it may interact with other medications.
